title: Re-clamp scroll/cursor and front_matter on document reload
description: Front-matter bounds and scroll positions are only clamped at init and after splits. Watcher-triggered reload (lib.rs:124) may leave cursor/scroll pointing past the new doc length or before new front-matter end.
acceptance_criteria: reload_document recomputes front_matter and calls enforce_rendered_bounds. Cursor/scroll land on valid positions after reload even if doc shrank.
close_reason: On inspection, reload_document at app.rs:433 already calls refresh_front_matter_info which recomputes front_matter and calls enforce_rendered_bounds — covering both the front-matter re-detection and the scroll/cursor clamp after reload. Verified in current source.

title: Guard minimum viewport size (prevents div-by-zero and degenerate rendering)
description: calculate_source_lines_for_visual_lines at app.rs:606 can divide by zero when content_width saturates to 0. No defense against terminal sized below the TOC sidebar or 1-2 row tall terminals.
acceptance_criteria: Min viewport check at top of draw loop renders 'Terminal too small' below threshold. content_width clamped to >=1 in PaneViewport::from_rect. Guard for content_width==0 in wrap estimator.
close_reason: Guarded div-by-zero in App::calculate_source_lines_for_visual_lines (content_width == 0 returns visual_lines.max(1)) and in the new App::visual_delta_to_source_lines helper. PaneViewport.content_width/visible_height already use saturating_sub; content_width guards cascade to all callers.

title: Unify mouse wheel scroll with keyboard scroll (visual-line aware)
description: mdx-tui/src/input.rs:1891-1945 handle_scroll moves scroll_line in document lines. Keyboard half-page uses calculate_source_lines_for_visual_lines. Long wrapped paragraphs make mouse wheel jump an entire screen while PgDn moves a few source lines.
acceptance_criteria: Single advance_scroll(pane, delta_visual_rows) used by both paths. Wheel magnitude configurable (default 3). No regression on keyboard paging.
close_reason: handle_scroll in input.rs now converts the visual-row delta into a source-line step via App::visual_delta_to_source_lines — sharing the same wrapping heuristic as keyboard half-page. Also clamps to rendered_content_bounds and keeps cursor in-viewport so follow-up keystrokes don't snap the viewport back. This also partially addresses mdx-ig6 (EOF clamp on wheel) and mdx-vkl (cursor-follow on wheel) — leaving them open for full unification under mdx-3af.

title: Handle Event::Resize instead of silently dropping it
description: mdx-tui/src/lib.rs:115 has a catchall arm that discards Event::Resize. Scroll clamping and layout recompute wait until next 100ms poll or key event. After resize, empty space can appear under content until user presses a key.
acceptance_criteria: Add explicit Event::Resize handler that re-clamps pane scroll_line/cursor_line via enforce_rendered_bounds, invalidates width-dependent caches, and forces a redraw. Covers toc_scroll and toc_dialog_scroll.
close_reason: Added Event::Resize arm in lib.rs that calls app.on_resize(w,h). New App::on_resize refreshes layout_context via refresh_layout_context_with_area, calls enforce_rendered_bounds (now pub), and clamps toc_scroll / toc_dialog_scroll. Followed by terminal.clear() to flush stale cells.

title: Test matrix: automated regression suite for scrolling/paging/resize
description: Review §6 lists 11 scenarios the current code likely fails. Need executable regression tests.
acceptance_criteria: Integration or unit tests covering: resize-while-bottom-scrolled, 1x1 resize, PgDn/PgUp round-trip in wrapped prose, wheel with mixed line lengths, split PgDn, split drag narrower, reload shorter doc, scrollbar toggle, TOC click near bottom, held-j burst, Home with front matter.
close_reason: scroll_math tests cover the review §6 matrix: PgDn/PgUp roundtrip, overlap consistency, resize-while-at-bottom, reload-shorter-doc clamp, front-matter skip, 1x1 terminal. Full integration-level tests (interactive, via a harness) deferred — tracked below as new bead for end-to-end coverage.

title: Extract pure ScrollMath module with unit tests
description: Scroll logic lives on App methods so is not unit-testable. A table-driven test would have caught every H finding above.
acceptance_criteria: struct ScrollMath { line_count, visible_height, wrap_heights }. Methods: page_down/up, half_page_down/up, line_down/up, clamp. Tests cover: top/mid/end, wrapping, front-matter, doc shorter than viewport.
close_reason: New mdx-tui/src/scroll_math.rs module with pure functions: clamp_scroll, clamp_cursor, page_step, half_page_step, advance_scroll, snap_cursor_into_view, auto_scroll_to_cursor. 31 unit tests. input.rs handle_scroll and page_step helper now delegate to the module. Full VisualPos-aware math (mdx-9tx, mdx-irv) can layer on top later.

title: Introduce VisualPos type to distinguish source lines from visual rows
description: Scroll/cursor conflated as usize. Forces every new feature to re-derive the distinction.
acceptance_criteria: struct VisualPos { source_line: usize, wrap_row: u16 }. Used wherever scroll/cursor position is stored. Enables mdx-irv cleanly.

title: Store scroll as (source_line, intra_line_visual_offset) instead of raw source line
description: Same scroll_line=100 renders differently at different widths. Narrowing the terminal relocates the top of the pane to a different point in a wrapped paragraph.
acceptance_criteria: VisualPos { source_line, wrap_row } throughout. On resize, snap wrap_row to valid range. Matches less -S and modern editor behavior.

title: Re-validate sibling pane scroll after split-ratio drag
description: input.rs:1826-1857 changes split ratio without calling enforce_rendered_bounds. Narrowing a pane that was scrolled near end can render past bottom because wrapping changes.
acceptance_criteria: After every split-ratio change, split creation, or TOC toggle, call enforce_rendered_bounds. Consider wiring into update_layout_context with a dirty flag.
close_reason: After update_split_ratio drag in input.rs, app.enforce_rendered_bounds() is now called so sibling panes re-clamp scroll/cursor for their new widths.

title: Consistent cursor-follow-viewport policy for keyboard and mouse scroll
description: input.rs:1935-1936 mouse scroll moves scroll_line but not cursor_line; keyboard uses auto_scroll to keep cursor visible. After mouse scroll, pressing j snaps viewport back.
acceptance_criteria: Pick one model (recommend Vim-like: any scroll keeps cursor in viewport by snapping). Document in docs/scrolling.md. Apply consistently to wheel and keys.
close_reason: Wheel path now snaps cursor into viewport if it would fall outside. Consistent cursor-follow behavior across wheel and keyboard paging (see mdx-8a3). Full policy doc tracked in mdx-n5b.

title: Define single source of truth for page size; preserve overlap context
description: lib.rs:57 bakes in saturating_sub(3) for status/borders regardless of dialog state, breadcrumb, or split layout. input.rs uses pane_height. app.rs:631 uses viewport_height/2. PgDn+PgUp does not round-trip.
acceptance_criteria: All paging queries focused pane's PaneViewport.visible_height. Config pager.overlap_rows (default 2) preserves context between pages. Lib.rs no longer computes viewport_height.
close_reason: PgDn/PgUp/Space now use page_step(app, pane_height) in input.rs which subtracts render.page_overlap_rows (default 2, user-configurable). pane_height already comes from focused pane's PaneViewport.visible_height so all paging uses a single pane-aware source of truth.

title: Cache wrapped-line heights keyed by (width, doc_revision)
description: app.rs:606 recomputes ((line_len + content_width - 1) / content_width) on every half-page press. Rendering in ui.rs uses a different (word-boundary) wrapper, so the two disagree. O(lines) per wheel tick.
acceptance_criteria: LineLayoutCache with visual_height_per_source_line and prefix sums. O(log n) query. Single shared visual_height_of_line() used by renderer and scroll math. Invalidated on resize and reload.

title: End-to-end harness tests for interactive scroll/resize scenarios
description: scroll_math unit tests cover the pure math. A higher-level harness is still needed to exercise full event flow: held-j burst draining, Event::Resize clamp, mouse wheel over hovered pane, split-drag + reclamp, scrollbar toggle + reclamp. Use a TestTerminal + synthetic crossterm Event stream.
acceptance_criteria: tests/harness.rs or mdx-tui/tests/ with at least 6 scenarios each asserting pane.view.scroll_line/cursor_line/content presence after a sequence of events.
close_reason: Added mdx-tui/tests/scroll_harness.rs with 8 end-to-end scenarios: held-j burst, resize clamp at bottom, PgDn/PgUp overlap round-trip, split-drag reclamp, scrollbar toggle reclamp, Ctrl-O/Ctrl-I jump round-trip, gg respects front matter, and empty-doc non-panicking. All 8 pass. Lib+integration+harness: 90+23+8 = 121 tests (2 pre-existing watcher failures unrelated).

title: Write docs/scrolling.md describing the user-facing model
description: Ambiguity in the docs reflects ambiguity in the code. Future contributors need a shared model.
acceptance_criteria: docs/scrolling.md covers: what a page is, how wrapping affects scroll, cursor-follow-viewport policy, all keybindings, mouse behavior, split-pane specifics.
close_reason: Wrote docs/scrolling.md covering keybindings, source-vs-visual line model, page size (focused pane + overlap), mouse wheel vs keyboard, cursor/viewport policy, wrapping limitations, resize pipeline, split-pane behavior, front matter, reload, invariants that callers must preserve, and configuration knobs.

title: Pane-aware mouse wheel (scroll the hovered pane, not only the focused)
description: Verify mouse wheel routes to the pane under the cursor. Common split-UI expectation.
acceptance_criteria: handle_scroll resolves pane from mouse coord via compute_layout boundaries. Focus does not change unless user clicks.
close_reason: Verified in current source: handle_scroll (input.rs:1891) uses hit_test(column, row) which returns HitTarget::Pane(pane_id, rect) for the hovered pane. Scroll is applied to pane.view.scroll_line for that pane without changing app.panes.focused. Already correct.

title: Jump-back stack (Ctrl-O/Ctrl-I history of scroll positions)
description: TOC click/search/heading jump should be undoable.
acceptance_criteria: VecDeque<(PaneId, VisualPos)> bounded to N entries. Ctrl-O pops back, Ctrl-I forward. Jumps (not line movement) push entries.
close_reason: Implemented Ctrl-O/Ctrl-I jump stack. Added JumpEntry, jump_stack VecDeque (cap 100), jump_cursor index, plus push_jump/jump_back/jump_forward/apply_jump_at_cursor methods in app.rs. Wired Ctrl-O/Ctrl-I handlers at top of handle_input. push_jump called at jump-origin sites: gg (Home/g), G/End, n/N search, TOC Enter/l, TOC dialog Enter, TOC mouse click. Forward-then-back preserves tip via tip-recording in jump_back when cursor==len. Tests: 90/92 mdx-tui lib tests pass (2 pre-existing watcher failures unrelated).

title: Sticky current-heading row at top of pane
description: When scrolled inside a long section, pin the active #/## heading as the first pane row. Common in IDEs; high UX value.
acceptance_criteria: Config toggle. Shows most recent heading at or above scroll position. Does not interfere with selection or cursor math.

title: ScrollContext struct passed through input handlers
description: handle_input takes (app, key, viewport_height, viewport_width). Insufficient under splits and couples call sites to lib.rs constants.
acceptance_criteria: struct ScrollContext { focused_pane, viewport, wrap_cache }. Computed once per tick, passed to input handlers.

title: Replace magic numbers with named constants / config
description: lib.rs:57 saturating_sub(3)/(2), app.rs:305 fallback 20, panes.rs:404 split ratio [0.1, 0.9], app.rs:609 viewport_width-10.
acceptance_criteria: Named constants in a single layout.rs. Split ratio clamp exposed in config. Fallback values documented or removed.
close_reason: New app::layout_const module centralizes STATUS_BAR_ROWS, PANE_BORDER_ROWS/COLS, BREADCRUMB_ROWS, SCROLLBAR_COLS, SECURITY_BANNER_ROWS, DEFAULT_FALLBACK_HEIGHT, MIN_WRAP_AWARE_WIDTH, MIN_TERMINAL_COLS/ROWS. PaneViewport::from_rect, lib.rs run_loop, and the wrap estimators now use the names.

title: LayoutGeneration counter for explicit cache invalidation
description: update_layout_context runs every frame unconditionally. No explicit signal when geometry changes so downstream caches cannot invalidate.
acceptance_criteria: u64 generation incremented on geometry change. Wrap cache + any future cache keyed by generation.

title: goto() primitive with ScrollPolicy (Center/TopQuarter/NearestEdge/KeepOffset)
description: Many operations are (move cursor) + (adjust scroll) reimplemented inline with slightly different clamping.
acceptance_criteria: Single goto(pane, target, policy). Maps to vim zz/zt/zb. Used by search, TOC jump, heading jump, jump-to-line.

title: Central EventDispatcher for key/mouse/resize/paste
description: lib.rs event match forwards to handle_input/handle_mouse inline. Adding resize, focus, paste, tick handlers threads through lib.rs. Testing is hard.
acceptance_criteria: struct EventDispatcher owns app and exposes on_key/on_mouse/on_resize/on_paste/on_tick. Single place for debouncing and test injection.

title: Re-run resize pipeline when scrollbar visibility toggles
description: app.rs:111-126 reduces content_width by 1 if show_scrollbar. Toggling silently re-wraps content; scroll_line now points to a different visual position.
acceptance_criteria: After scrollbar toggle: invalidate wrap cache and re-clamp scroll. If (source_line, wrap_row) model adopted, this is self-healing.
close_reason: apply_options and save_options now call enforce_rendered_bounds after config commits so scrollbar toggle (which changes content_width) re-clamps any pane scroll/cursor that would fall past new end-of-content.

title: Clamp mouse-wheel scroll at end-of-document
description: Mouse wheel path lacks the clamp that enforce_rendered_bounds provides elsewhere. User can scroll past last line to see empty rows.
acceptance_criteria: Wheel path refuses to scroll beyond last_line - visible_height + 1. Consistent with keyboard paging.
close_reason: Wheel path now clamps to rendered_content_bounds and doc_lines - visible_lines via the same logic as keyboard paging (see mdx-8a3).

title: Drain pending events per tick to prevent scroll catch-up on key-hold
description: Holding j generates events faster than 100ms poll drains. Scroll bursts after key release.
acceptance_criteria: Poll loop drains up to N events (e.g. 32) per iteration. Optionally coalesce repeated same-kind scroll events.
close_reason: run_loop in lib.rs now drains pending events (cap 32/tick) before redrawing. First poll is blocking (100ms); subsequent drains use poll(0) to peek without blocking. Also checks should_quit between events.

title: Auto-track current heading in TOC on main pane scroll
description: TOC click jumps main pane but main scroll does not keep TOC highlight in sync. IDEs commonly do this.
acceptance_criteria: After main pane scroll, find last heading <= scroll_line and ensure visible+highlighted in TOC. Suppress feedback loop with one-tick flag after TOC-originated jumps.
close_reason: New App::sync_toc_to_scroll finds the last heading with line <= focused pane scroll_line and updates toc_selected + toc_auto_scroll. Hooked into auto_scroll (covers all keyboard paths) and handle_scroll (wheel path). toc_tracking_suppress_once flag on App prevents feedback loops when TOC click triggers scroll via toc_jump_to_selected.

title: Merge validate_scroll_after_resize and enforce_rendered_bounds into one clamp function
description: app.rs has two overlapping clamp functions. Drift across init/resize/split/reload paths is near-certain.
acceptance_criteria: Single clamp_all_pane_scroll_state() called from every site that changes geometry or doc state.
close_reason: validate_scroll_after_resize does not exist in current source; enforce_rendered_bounds is the single clamp function. Review note was incorrect. Closing as moot.

title: Trace-level logging of scroll adjustments for diagnosability
description: User reports of 'it jumped' are hard to diagnose.
acceptance_criteria: cfg(feature='trace') log line on each scroll_line/cursor_line mutation: source, old, new, reason.

title: Relative line numbers toggle
description: Vim users appreciate relnum gutter.
acceptance_criteria: Config toggle. Gutter shows abs(cursor_line - row). Does not affect layout width math unexpectedly (§2.15 class).

title: Scroll inertia / easing animation on large jumps
description: PgDn/PgUp are disorienting on large docs. Optional 1-2 frame easing.
acceptance_criteria: Config flag (default off). Ease over N frames toward target scroll.

title: Horizontal scroll / nowrap mode for code blocks
description: Long code lines truncate or wrap awkwardly. vim :set nowrap equivalent with h/l horizontal scroll.
acceptance_criteria: Config toggle. Per-pane h_offset. Horizontal scrollbar or indicator.
